target.name: Majestic Labs
target.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $11B

data.narrative:
Majestic Labs is a Series A-stage chip design company founded by Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, and Masumi Reynders, having raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 private frontier-chip companies and 3 public semiconductor peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), with particular focus on same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables including SambaNova Systems, Etched, and Positron AI.

Two valuation methods triangulate a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $100M raised proxy, implying $200M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Positron AI, Series B) to $11.0B (SambaNova Systems, Series A). The wide peer dispersion reflects early-stage valuation volatility in frontier chip markets, where funding stage alone does not tightly anchor value.

Triangulation across both methods supports a range of $170M to $5.8B, with a median of $2.6B. This wide band reflects material uncertainty around technology validation, market timing, and whether Majestic's architecture will achieve competitive parity or premium positioning relative to established and emerging competitors. The peer-median methodology carries greater weight given the stage-progression floor's distance from recent market activity, but both methods should be stress-tested against concrete product milestones and customer validation before narrowing the range further.
